description:
## Question

Does the cask-installed SuperWhisper ship the hook binary the reference relies on, and how do we invoke it from a Nix-generated hook command?

Confirm:
- Exact path (reference hardcodes /Applications/superwhisper.app/Contents/Resources/claude-hook) and whether the cask installs to /Applications.
- What it reads from stdin (the Claude Code hook JSON payload) and what it does (fires superwhisper://agent-update deeplink? opens the app?).
- Minimum SuperWhisper version (README says 2.13+).
- Whether the binary honors the per-project disable flag (/tmp/superwhisper-agent/disabled-<md5(pwd)>) and/or SUPERWHISPER_AGENT=0, or whether those are display-only in the reference statusline. This determines whether the toggle skill actually gates behavior.
- Any TCC/permission prerequisites (mic, accessibility).

Inspect the locally-installed app if present. AFK research; produce a linked markdown summary.

comments.text:
RESOLVED (research). Full findings: docs/research/superwhisper-claude-hook.md (committed nix-4t3.1).

Key answers:
- Path CONFIRMED: /Applications/superwhisper.app/Contents/Resources/claude-hook. Homebrew cask 'superwhisper' installs superwhisper.app as an App artifact -> /Applications. (Cask auto_updates=true; path stable, version drifts.)
- claude-hook is a 56-byte shim: 'exec $(dirname $0)/agent-hook claude $@'. Real logic = agent-hook Mach-O binary.
- stdin = Claude Code hook JSON (hook_event_name, session_id, transcript_path, stop_hook_active, notification_type, permission_*). Multi-event dispatcher; reference wires 5 hooks to the same command: Stop, Notification, PreToolUse(AskUserQuestion), PermissionRequest, UserPromptSubmit. On Stop fires superwhisper://agent-update deeplink. Side effect: writes always-allow rules to per-project .claude/settings.local.json.
- Version: installed 2.16.5, cask 2.16.6 >= 2.13 required. OK.
- GATING (decides toggle skill): the /tmp/superwhisper-agent/disabled-<md5(cwd)> file gates the BINARY (strings: 'Exiting: superwhisper disabled for cwd=' / 'for session='). REAL, not display-only. md5 is over $PWD (cwd, not git root). SUPERWHISPER_AGENT=0 is DISPLAY-ONLY (only in statusline.sh, not in agent-hook) -> toggle skill (nix-4t3.4) must write the /tmp file, not the env var. Reference skill does exactly this.
- TCC: Microphone + Accessibility, granted manually to the app (not hook-level, not Nix-configurable). Feeds map's 'Not yet specified' TCC note.

title: Verify SuperWhisper's claude-hook binary and its interface
